Home page/Industries/Telecommunications

Telecommunications Equipment Parts Manufacturing Support

Support for communication equipment housings, heat sinks, mounting structures, RF-related mechanical parts, brackets and low-volume verification components.

Equipment housingsHeat sinksMounting structuresLow-volume verification

Mechanical manufacturing for communication equipment

Communication equipment parts need stable structure, heat management, surface durability and assembly consistency. Manufacturing review is based on drawings, materials and order requirements.

Housings and mounting parts

Manufacture covers, frames, brackets, plates and mounting structures for equipment assemblies.

Thermal components

Support machined heat sinks and structural thermal paths when drawings define geometry and material.

Surface protection

Coordinate anodizing, painting, passivation and marking according to use environment.

Manufacturing Priorities

01
Dimensional consistencyReview hole patterns, mounting interfaces and inspection references for repeatable assembly.
02
Heat and material choicesConfirm aluminum, copper, stainless steel or engineering plastic options by requirement.
03
Outdoor or enclosure needsConfirm coating, sealing-related surfaces and corrosion resistance expectations.

Review Checklist

No commitment to vehicle regulatory certificationClearly express the purpose of the sampleMaterial performance needs to be confirmed by actual measurementThe delivery date is subject to the quotation

Submit telecommunications part requirements for review

Provide drawings, materials, quantities and finish requirements to start manufacturing evaluation.

Start assessment
